evals.name: scrollable-list-app
evals.prompt: Using Rust and ratatui, write me a small terminal app: a bordered box titled "Tasks" containing a scrollable, selectable list of strings, with a one-line footer at the bottom showing the keybindings. Up/Down (and j/k) move the selection, q or Esc quits. Give me a complete main.rs I can cargo run, and a Cargo.toml.
evals.expected_output: A complete, compiling ratatui 0.30 app using List + ListState, a Layout with a footer, as_key_press_event filtering, ratatui::run.

evals.name: llm-chat-input
evals.prompt: I'm building an LLM agent CLI in Rust with ratatui. I need the message-input box at the bottom of the screen: it should grow vertically as the user types more lines (up to ~6 rows), send the message when they press Enter, and insert a newline on Alt+Enter instead of sending. Pasting a multi-line block should not trigger a send. Show me how to build the input widget and wire the key handling.
evals.expected_output: Auto-growing input via wrapped-line height + Layout, Enter-to-send vs Alt+Enter newline, bracketed paste handling, set_cursor_position each frame. Matches the 06-text-editing chat input.

evals.name: custom-widget
evals.prompt: In ratatui (latest), how do I write a reusable custom widget? I want a labeled status indicator I can keep in my app state and render each frame from a &self reference (so rendering doesn't consume it). Give me the trait impl and how I'd render it.
evals.expected_output: impl Widget for &MyWidget (the 0.30 idiom), NOT WidgetRef. Rendered via frame.render_widget(&self.x, area).
